On November 25, 2025, Ascendis Pharma announced that the FDA has extended the PDUFA target action date for the New Drug Application of TransCon CNP for children with achondroplasia by three months to February 28, 2026. The delay may impact Ascendis’ timeline for bringing this treatment to market, potentially affecting its competitive positioning in the biopharmaceutical industry.

data.title: Fluence Energy Reports 2025 Results and 2026 Outlook
data.description:
Fluence Energy Inc Class A ( (FLNC) ) has released its Q4 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Fluence Energy Inc Class A presented to its investors.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Fluence Energy, Inc., a global leader in energy storage and optimization software, reported its financial results for the fiscal year 2025, highlighting its role in transforming energy markets with intelligent solutions. The company operates in the renewable energy sector, focusing on energy storage systems and asset optimization software to enhance grid resilience and unlock renewable energy potential.

In its latest earnings report, Fluence Energy announced a revenue of $2.3 billion for fiscal year 2025, with a fourth-quarter revenue of $1.0 billion. Despite a decrease from the previous year, the company achieved a record order intake of over $1.4 billion in the fourth quarter and improved its gross profit margin to 13.1% for the year.

Key financial metrics revealed a net loss of $68.0 million for the fiscal year, although the company posted a net income of $24.1 million in the fourth quarter. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $19.5 million, with a significant increase to $72.2 million in the fourth quarter. The company’s backlog reached a record $5.3 billion, and its liquidity position improved to $1.3 billion, the highest in its history.

Looking ahead, Fluence Energy has set its fiscal year 2026 revenue guidance between $3.2 billion and $3.6 billion, with a midpoint of $3.4 billion. The company anticipates an adjusted EBITDA of $40.0 million to $60.0 million, supported by a strong backlog covering 85% of its revenue guidance. The management remains optimistic about capitalizing on the growing demand for energy storage solutions.

With a robust backlog and strategic positioning in the energy storage market, Fluence Energy is poised for significant growth in the coming year, driven by its commitment to innovation and market expansion.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Newsdesk

In its latest earnings report for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2025, Alico announced a net loss of $147.3 million, attributed mainly to accelerated depreciation and asset impairments as part of its strategic transformation. Despite this, the company exceeded its adjusted EBITDA and land sales guidance, reporting $22.5 million and $23.8 million respectively. PagerDuty, Inc., a leader in digital operations management, offers an AI-powered platform that enhances business resilience and operational efficiency for enterprises. In its third quarter fiscal 2026 earnings report, PagerDuty announced a 5% year-over-year revenue increase to $125 million, marking its second consecutive quarter of GAAP profitability. The company also reported an annual recurring revenue growth of 3% to $497 million.

Key financial highlights include a GAAP operating income of $8 million and a non-GAAP operating income of $36 million. The net income per diluted share was $1.69, while the non-GAAP net income per diluted share stood at $0.33. PagerDuty’s cash, cash equivalents, and investments totaled $547.8 million as of October 31, 2025. The company also saw a 5% increase in customers with annual recurring revenue over $100,000, reaching 867 customers.

Strategically, PagerDuty launched the industry’s first end-to-end incident management AI Agent Suite, reducing incident response times and fostering innovation. The company also joined Glean’s AI Ecosystem as the first incident management partner and was recognized for its workplace culture and corporate social responsibility initiatives.

Looking ahead, PagerDuty anticipates fourth-quarter revenue growth of 0% to 2% year-over-year, with a non-GAAP net income per diluted share expected to be between $0.24 and $0.25. For the full fiscal year 2026, the company projects a revenue growth rate of 5% year-over-year, with non-GAAP net income per diluted share between $1.11 and $1.12. PagerDuty remains focused on advancing its pricing and go-to-market strategies to support sustainable growth.

Zscaler, Inc., a leading company in the cloud security sector, provides a Zero Trust Exchange platform that securely connects users, devices, and applications worldwide. In its first quarter of fiscal 2026, Zscaler reported a 26% year-over-year increase in revenue, reaching $788.1 million, and a similar growth in Annual Recurring Revenue to $3.204 billion. The company also achieved a non-GAAP net income of $159.5 million, up from $124.3 million the previous year, while maintaining a strong cash flow from operations. Key financial metrics highlighted include a 32% increase in deferred revenue and a robust free cash flow margin of 52%. Zscaler’s strategic initiatives included the acquisition of SPLXAI Inc., enhancing its AI security offerings, and launching new innovations in its Zscaler Digital Experience platform. Looking ahead, Zscaler’s management remains optimistic about continued growth, projecting revenue between $797 million and $799 million for the second quarter of fiscal 2026, with a focus on expanding its AI security capabilities and global presence.
